programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 6
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and eggbeater kick. Learn stride entries, surface dives, breaststroke and interval training.  Work towards a continuous swim of 150 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 5.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 6 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and eggbeater kick. Learn stride entries, surface dives, breaststroke and interval training. Work towards a continuous swim of 150 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 5.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 7
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and eggbeater kick. Learn stride entries, surface dives, breaststroke and interval training.  Work towards a continuous swim of 150 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 5.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 7
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives, eggbeater kick, front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and breaststroke. Learn shallow dives into deep water and treading water while fully clothed. Work towards a continuous swim of 200 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 6.
programs.location: Joseph J. Piccininni Cmty Rec Centre

programs.name: Ultra Swim 7
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives, eggbeater kick, front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and breaststroke. Learn shallow dives into deep water and treading water while fully clothed. Work towards a continuous swim of 200 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 6.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 8
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives with underwater swimming, interval training, object support and all strokes. Work towards a continuous swim of 300 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 8 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives with underwater swimming, interval training, object support and all strokes. Work towards a continuous swim of 300 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 8 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives with underwater swimming, interval training, object support and all strokes. Work towards a continuous swim of 300 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 8 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives with underwater swimming, interval training, object support and all strokes. Work towards a continuous swim of 300 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 9
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop non-contact rescues and stroke improvement. Learn stride jump. Work towards a continuous swim of 400 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 8.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 9
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop non-contact rescues and stroke improvement. Learn stride jump. Work towards a continuous swim of 400 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 8.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 9 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop non-contact rescues and stroke improvement. Learn stride jump. Work towards a continuous swim of 400 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 8.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 9 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop non-contact rescues and stroke improvement. Learn stride jump. Work towards a continuous swim of 400 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 8.

programs.name: Ultra Youth Swim 1 (Girls)
programs.description: Build confidence in the water and improve your swimming skills. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres. This activity is for all women. Be advised, no male caregivers or spectators will be allowed in the changerooms, in pool, on the pool deck or in the pool gallery during this activity.

programs.name: Volleyball: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In volleyball, a team sport where the objective is to hit the ball so it makes contact with the opposing teams court, you will learn basics like serving, volleying, bumping, spiking and passing as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Yoga
programs.description: Relax your body and mind with yoga. Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your balance, breathing, flexibility and strength.

programs.name: Yoga
programs.description: Relax your body and mind with yoga.  Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your balance, breathing, flexibility and strength.

programs.name: Yoga
programs.description: Relax your body and mind with yoga. Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your balance, breathing, flexibility and strength.

programs.name: Yoga
programs.description: Relax your body and mind with yoga. Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your balance, breathing, flexibility and strength.

programs.name: Yoga/Pilates Fusion
programs.description: Yoga and Pilates movements are combined to create a unique fitness experience. Focusing on body alignment and overall flexibility, you will learn fundamentals from both practices to improve your strength, coordination and relaxation.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Dance Basics
programs.description: Explore and enjoy a variety of dance styles including ballet, jazz, hip hop and contemporary. As you practice basic steps and proper technique, you will build on flexibility, strength and endurance.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your dance skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your drama skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics, or workshops to further enhance the camp program.

programs.description: Get ready to create. Using a variety of arts and crafts materials, skills and your imagination, you will make unique craft projects. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Dance Basics
programs.description: Explore and enjoy a variety of dance styles including ballet, jazz, hip hop and contemporary. As you practice basic steps and proper technique, you will build on flexibility, strength and endurance.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your dance skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Dance Basics
programs.description: Explore and enjoy a variety of dance styles including ballet, jazz, hip hop and contemporary. As you practice basic steps and proper technique, you will build on flexibility, strength and endurance.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your dance skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

As you practice basic steps and proper technique, you will build on flexibility, strength and endurance.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your dance skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Aquatic Games and Skills
programs.description: Playing games and having fun is a wonderful way to practice swimming skills. Designed for swimmers with a disability or special needs, structured aquatic games and activities such as water polo, synchronized swimming and aquatic fitness, provide the perfect oppourtunity to learn and build on basic swimming fundamentals.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Arts
programs.description: Welcome to the realm of abstract art, where linear rules and colour coordination pose no barriers to expression. Using various artistic mediums including paint, clay, food, textiles and water you will create wonderful works of art.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Basketball
programs.description: Focus on developing basketball skills while learning the importance of teamwork and cooperation. Learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play. Develop confidence in both your game and team play as you interact and have fun with peers. Participants must be able to function independently within the program ratio.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Boccia
programs.description: Focus on developing boccia skills while learning the importance of teamwork and cooperation. Learn the basics of this sport, which is similar to lawn bowling, including throwing, aiming, shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play. Develop confidence in both your game and team play as you interact and have fun with peers.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 6
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and eggbeater kick. Learn stride entries, surface dives, breaststroke and interval training.  Work towards a continuous swim of 150 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 5.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 6 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and eggbeater kick. Learn stride entries, surface dives, breaststroke and interval training. Work towards a continuous swim of 150 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 5.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 7
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and eggbeater kick. Learn stride entries, surface dives, breaststroke and interval training.  Work towards a continuous swim of 150 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 5.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 7
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives, eggbeater kick, front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and breaststroke. Learn shallow dives into deep water and treading water while fully clothed. Work towards a continuous swim of 200 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 6.
programs.location: Joseph J. Piccininni Cmty Rec Centre

programs.name: Ultra Swim 7
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives, eggbeater kick, front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and breaststroke. Learn shallow dives into deep water and treading water while fully clothed. Work towards a continuous swim of 200 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 6.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 8
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives with underwater swimming, interval training, object support and all strokes. Work towards a continuous swim of 300 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 8 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives with underwater swimming, interval training, object support and all strokes. Work towards a continuous swim of 300 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 8 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives with underwater swimming, interval training, object support and all strokes. Work towards a continuous swim of 300 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 8 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives with underwater swimming, interval training, object support and all strokes. Work towards a continuous swim of 300 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 9
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop non-contact rescues and stroke improvement. Learn stride jump. Work towards a continuous swim of 400 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 8.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 9
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop non-contact rescues and stroke improvement. Learn stride jump. Work towards a continuous swim of 400 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 8.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 9 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop non-contact rescues and stroke improvement. Learn stride jump. Work towards a continuous swim of 400 metres. programs.name: Ultra Swim 9 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop non-contact rescues and stroke improvement. Learn stride jump. Work towards a continuous swim of 400 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 8.

programs.name: Ultra Youth Swim 1 (Girls)
programs.description: Build confidence in the water and improve your swimming skills. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres. This activity is for all women. Be advised, no male caregivers or spectators will be allowed in the changerooms, in pool, on the pool deck or in the pool gallery during this activity.

programs.name: Volleyball: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In volleyball, a team sport where the objective is to hit the ball so it makes contact with the opposing teams court, you will learn basics like serving, volleying, bumping, spiking and passing as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Dance Basics
programs.description: Explore and enjoy a variety of dance styles including ballet, jazz, hip hop and contemporary. As you practice basic steps and proper technique, you will build on flexibility, strength and endurance.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your dance skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

In this camp you will learn basic moves from both disciplines and blend them with choreography and your favourite music.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your acro skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp program.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Aquatic Games and Skills
programs.description: Playing games and having fun is a wonderful way to practice swimming skills. Designed for swimmers with a disability or special needs, structured aquatic games and activities such as water polo, synchronized swimming and aquatic fitness, provide the perfect oppourtunity to learn and build on basic swimming fundamentals.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Arts
programs.description: Welcome to the realm of abstract art, where linear rules and colour coordination pose no barriers to expression. Using various artistic mediums including paint, clay, food, textiles and water you will create wonderful works of art.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Basketball
programs.description: Focus on developing basketball skills while learning the importance of teamwork and cooperation. Learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play. Develop confidence in both your game and team play as you interact and have fun with peers. Participants must be able to function independently within the program ratio.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.description: Focus on developing basketball skills while learning the importance of teamwork and cooperation. Participants must be able to function independently within the program ratio.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Boccia
programs.description: Focus on developing boccia skills while learning the importance of teamwork and cooperation. Learn the basics of this sport, which is similar to lawn bowling, including throwing, aiming, shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play. Develop confidence in both your game and team play as you interact and have fun with peers.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Golf: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills through play with recreational golf. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Swim Lessons - Private
programs.description: Do you want to develop specific skills at your own pace? This activity provides personal swim instruction with a one-on-one ratio.

programs.name: Yoga with Baby
programs.description: Relax your body and mind with yoga. Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your balance, breathing, flexibility and strength in this class specifically designed for new caregivers. Here you can relax knowing participation is at your own pace and you can attend to your baby as needed. The recommended age for your baby is 1 to 12 months.

programs.name: Yoga/Pilates Fusion
programs.description: Yoga and Pilates movements are combined to create a unique fitness experience. Focusing on body alignment and overall flexibility, you will learn fundamentals from both practices to improve your strength, coordination and relaxation.

programs.name: Adult Swim 2
programs.description: Build confidence in deep water one stroke at a time. Develop front crawl, back crawl, glides and staying afloat in deep water. Learn stride entry, eggbeater kick and breaststroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 50 to 100 meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Adult Swim 1.

programs.name: Adult Swim 3
programs.description: Build endurance in the water one stroke at a time. Develop staying afloat in water and stroke improvement. Work towards a continuous swim of 100 to 300 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Adult Swim 2.

programs.name: Dance: Line Dance
programs.description: Are you ready to step up your dance skills with line dancing? Using basic steps to create repetitive combinations, you'll learn moves like the shuffle, grapevine, coaster step and mambo step through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Line Dance
programs.description: Are you ready to step up your dance skills with line dancing? Using basic steps to create repetitive combinations, you'll learn moves like the shuffle, grapevine, coaster step and mambo step through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 2
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Develop front and back floats and staying afloat in the water. Learn front and back glide and going underwater. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 1.

programs.name: Jewellery Making
programs.description: Jewellery enthusiasts will love this activity. Here you will use beads, jewellery findings and wire to make a variety of projects. You will also learn how to use the tools of the trade to design your own masterpieces.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Weight Train - Small Group
programs.description: Strengthen your muscles with weight training. Perfect for beginners, you will learn fundamental techniques and a variety of exercises to help enhance your strength, mobility, flexibility and stamina safely. Class size is limited to four participants.

programs.name: Osteo Fit
programs.description: Help build and maintain stronger bones by increasing muscle strength with Osteo Fit. Suitable for individuals with low bone density or osteoporosis, exercises consist of slow-paced, weight-bearing movements with resistance.

programs.name: Painting: Watercolour
programs.description: Create pictures with watercolour paint. Learn basic brush techniques as well as how to choose colours, build layers to show depth and shadow and apply washes for unique effects.

programs.name: Painting: Watercolour
programs.description: Create pictures with watercolour paint. Learn basic brush techniques as well as how to choose colours, build layers to show depth and shadow and apply washes for unique effects.

programs.name: Tiny Tots Swim
programs.description: Would your little one enjoy splashing around and getting comfortable in the water at their own pace? This 15-minute activity provides personal swim instruction with a one-on-one ratio.

programs.name: Adult Swim 1
programs.description: Build confidence in the water one stroke at a time. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres.

programs.name: Computers
programs.description: Designed for first-time users or those with some knowledge, this introduction to computers focuses on how to use a device with confidence. Learn basic computer skills like navigation as well as how to create an email, access the web, create documents and more.Equipment: Use of computers vary by location, please check ahead. You may bring a laptop.

programs.name: Drawing
programs.description: Bring your imagination to life on paper. Learn the basics of how to draw using shapes and colours to create pictures with markers, pencils, and pencil crayons.

programs.name: Functional Fit
programs.description: This conditioning workout is designed to help you build strength to support day-to-day activities. At your own pace, you will practice basic movements like pushing, pulling, bending and squatting to improve your overall functional fitness.

Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Golf: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills through play with recreational golf. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Swim Lessons - Private
programs.description: Do you want to develop specific skills at your own pace? This activity provides personal swim instruction with a one-on-one ratio.

programs.name: Yoga with Baby
programs.description: Relax your body and mind with yoga. Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your balance, breathing, flexibility and strength in this class specifically designed for new caregivers. Here you can relax knowing participation is at your own pace and you can attend to your baby as needed. The recommended age for your baby is 1 to 12 months.

programs.name: Yoga/Pilates Fusion
programs.description: Yoga and Pilates movements are combined to create a unique fitness experience. Focusing on body alignment and overall flexibility, you will learn fundamentals from both practices to improve your strength, coordination and relaxation.

programs.name: Adult Swim 2
programs.description: Build confidence in deep water one stroke at a time. Develop front crawl, back crawl, glides and staying afloat in deep water. Learn stride entry, eggbeater kick and breaststroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 50 to 100 meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Adult Swim 1.

programs.name: Adult Swim 3
programs.description: Build endurance in the water one stroke at a time. Develop staying afloat in water and stroke improvement. Work towards a continuous swim of 100 to 300 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Adult Swim 2.

programs.name: Dance: Line Dance
programs.description: Are you ready to step up your dance skills with line dancing? Using basic steps to create repetitive combinations, you'll learn moves like the shuffle, grapevine, coaster step and mambo step through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Line Dance
programs.description: Are you ready to step up your dance skills with line dancing? Using basic steps to create repetitive combinations, you'll learn moves like the shuffle, grapevine, coaster step and mambo step through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 2
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Develop front and back floats and staying afloat in the water. Learn front and back glide and going underwater. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 1.

programs.name: Jewellery Making
programs.description: Jewellery enthusiasts will love this activity. Here you will use beads, jewellery findings and wire to make a variety of projects. You will also learn how to use the tools of the trade to design your own masterpieces.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Weight Train - Small Group
programs.description: Strengthen your muscles with weight training. Perfect for beginners, you will learn fundamental techniques and a variety of exercises to help enhance your strength, mobility, flexibility and stamina safely. Class size is limited to four participants.

programs.name: Osteo Fit
programs.description: Help build and maintain stronger bones by increasing muscle strength with Osteo Fit. Suitable for individuals with low bone density or osteoporosis, exercises consist of slow-paced, weight-bearing movements with resistance.

programs.name: Painting: Watercolour
programs.description: Create pictures with watercolour paint. Learn basic brush techniques as well as how to choose colours, build layers to show depth and shadow and apply washes for unique effects.

programs.name: Painting: Watercolour
programs.description: Create pictures with watercolour paint. Learn basic brush techniques as well as how to choose colours, build layers to show depth and shadow and apply washes for unique effects.

programs.name: Tiny Tots Swim
programs.description: Would your little one enjoy splashing around and getting comfortable in the water at their own pace? This 15-minute activity provides personal swim instruction with a one-on-one ratio.

programs.name: Adult Swim 1
programs.description: Build confidence in the water one stroke at a time. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres.

programs.name: Computers
programs.description: Designed for first-time users or those with some knowledge, this introduction to computers focuses on how to use a device with confidence. Learn basic computer skills like navigation as well as how to create an email, access the web, create documents and more.Equipment: Use of computers vary by location, please check ahead. You may bring a laptop.

programs.name: Drawing
programs.description: Bring your imagination to life on paper. Learn the basics of how to draw using shapes and colours to create pictures with markers, pencils, and pencil crayons.

programs.name: Functional Fit
programs.description: This conditioning workout is designed to help you build strength to support day-to-day activities. At your own pace, you will practice basic movements like pushing, pulling, bending and squatting to improve your overall functional fitness.

programs.description: Bring your imagination to life on paper. Learn the basics of how to draw using shapes and colours to create pictures with markers, pencils, and pencil crayons.

programs.name: Functional Fit
programs.description: This conditioning workout is designed to help you build strength to support day-to-day activities. At your own pace, you will practice basic movements like pushing, pulling, bending and squatting to improve your overall functional fitness.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Drama
programs.description: Step into the spotlight and take part in the mysteries of the theatre through drama coaching, set designs and dramatic performances. Learn and practice drama techniques like improv and role-playing, voice projection, expression and script interpretation via skits and exercises. In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your drama skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Drawing and Sketching
programs.description: Bring your imagination to life with pen and paper. Campers will learn the fundamentals of drawing and sketching with a focus on developing skills such as perspective and shading using values and tones. In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your drawing and sketching skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp program.

programs.location: Joseph J. Piccininni Cmty Rec Centre

programs.name: Golf: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills through play with recreational golf. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Swim Lessons - Private
programs.description: Do you want to develop specific skills at your own pace? This activity provides personal swim instruction with a one-on-one ratio.

programs.name: Yoga with Baby
programs.description: Relax your body and mind with yoga. Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your balance, breathing, flexibility and strength in this class specifically designed for new caregivers. Here you can relax knowing participation is at your own pace and you can attend to your baby as needed. The recommended age for your baby is 1 to 12 months.

programs.name: Yoga/Pilates Fusion
programs.description: Yoga and Pilates movements are combined to create a unique fitness experience. Focusing on body alignment and overall flexibility, you will learn fundamentals from both practices to improve your strength, coordination and relaxation.

programs.name: Adult Swim 2
programs.description: Build confidence in deep water one stroke at a time. Develop front crawl, back crawl, glides and staying afloat in deep water. Learn stride entry, eggbeater kick and breaststroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 50 to 100 meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Adult Swim 1.

programs.name: Adult Swim 3
programs.description: Build endurance in the water one stroke at a time. Develop staying afloat in water and stroke improvement. Work towards a continuous swim of 100 to 300 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Adult Swim 2.

programs.name: Dance: Line Dance
programs.description: Are you ready to step up your dance skills with line dancing? Using basic steps to create repetitive combinations, you'll learn moves like the shuffle, grapevine, coaster step and mambo step through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Line Dance
programs.description: Are you ready to step up your dance skills with line dancing? Using basic steps to create repetitive combinations, you'll learn moves like the shuffle, grapevine, coaster step and mambo step through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 2
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Develop front and back floats and staying afloat in the water. Learn front and back glide and going underwater. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 1.

programs.name: Jewellery Making
programs.description: Jewellery enthusiasts will love this activity. Here you will use beads, jewellery findings and wire to make a variety of projects. You will also learn how to use the tools of the trade to design your own masterpieces.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Weight Train - Small Group
programs.description: Strengthen your muscles with weight training. Perfect for beginners, you will learn fundamental techniques and a variety of exercises to help enhance your strength, mobility, flexibility and stamina safely. Class size is limited to four participants.

programs.name: Osteo Fit
programs.description: Help build and maintain stronger bones by increasing muscle strength with Osteo Fit. Suitable for individuals with low bone density or osteoporosis, exercises consist of slow-paced, weight-bearing movements with resistance.

programs.name: Painting: Watercolour
programs.description: Create pictures with watercolour paint. Learn basic brush techniques as well as how to choose colours, build layers to show depth and shadow and apply washes for unique effects.

programs.name: Painting: Watercolour
programs.description: Create pictures with watercolour paint. Learn basic brush techniques as well as how to choose colours, build layers to show depth and shadow and apply washes for unique effects.

programs.name: Tiny Tots Swim
programs.description: Would your little one enjoy splashing around and getting comfortable in the water at their own pace? This 15-minute activity provides personal swim instruction with a one-on-one ratio.

programs.name: Adult Swim 1
programs.description: Build confidence in the water one stroke at a time. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres.

programs.name: Computers
programs.description: Designed for first-time users or those with some knowledge, this introduction to computers focuses on how to use a device with confidence. Learn basic computer skills like navigation as well as how to create an email, access the web, create documents and more.Equipment: Use of computers vary by location, please check ahead. You may bring a laptop.

programs.name: Drawing
programs.description: Bring your imagination to life on paper. Learn the basics of how to draw using shapes and colours to create pictures with markers, pencils, and pencil crayons.

programs.name: Functional Fit
programs.description: This conditioning workout is designed to help you build strength to support day-to-day activities. At your own pace, you will practice basic movements like pushing, pulling, bending and squatting to improve your overall functional fitness.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.description: Bring your imagination to life on paper. Learn the basics of how to draw using shapes and colours to create pictures with markers, pencils, and pencil crayons.

programs.name: Functional Fit
programs.description: This conditioning workout is designed to help you build strength to support day-to-day activities. At your own pace, you will practice basic movements like pushing, pulling, bending and squatting to improve your overall functional fitness.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.description: Welcome to the realm of abstract art, where linear rules and colour coordination pose no barriers to expression. Using various artistic mediums including paint, clay, food, textiles and water you will create wonderful works of art.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Basketball
programs.description: Focus on developing basketball skills while learning the importance of teamwork and cooperation. Learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play. Develop confidence in both your game and team play as you interact and have fun with peers. Participants must be able to function independently within the program ratio.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Boccia
programs.description: Focus on developing boccia skills while learning the importance of teamwork and cooperation. Develop confidence in both your game and team play as you interact and have fun with peers.Participants must have a current AIS membership. programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Drama
programs.description: Step into the spotlight and take part in the mysteries of the theatre through drama coaching, set designs and dramatic performances. Learn and practice drama techniques like improv and role-playing, voice projection, expression and script interpretation via skits and exercises. In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your drama skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Drawing and Sketching
programs.description: Bring your imagination to life with pen and paper. Campers will learn the fundamentals of drawing and sketching with a focus on developing skills such as perspective and shading using values and tones. In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your drawing and sketching skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp program.

programs.name: Golf: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills through play with recreational golf. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Swim Lessons - Private
programs.description: Do you want to develop specific skills at your own pace? This activity provides personal swim instruction with a one-on-one ratio.

programs.name: Yoga with Baby
programs.description: Relax your body and mind with yoga. Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your balance, breathing, flexibility and strength in this class specifically designed for new caregivers. Here you can relax knowing participation is at your own pace and you can attend to your baby as needed. The recommended age for your baby is 1 to 12 months.

programs.name: Yoga/Pilates Fusion
programs.description: Yoga and Pilates movements are combined to create a unique fitness experience. Focusing on body alignment and overall flexibility, you will learn fundamentals from both practices to improve your strength, coordination and relaxation.

programs.name: Adult Swim 2
programs.description: Build confidence in deep water one stroke at a time. Develop front crawl, back crawl, glides and staying afloat in deep water. Learn stride entry, eggbeater kick and breaststroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 50 to 100 meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Adult Swim 1.

programs.name: Adult Swim 3
programs.description: Build endurance in the water one stroke at a time. Develop staying afloat in water and stroke improvement. Work towards a continuous swim of 100 to 300 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Adult Swim 2.

programs.name: Dance: Line Dance
programs.description: Are you ready to step up your dance skills with line dancing? Using basic steps to create repetitive combinations, you'll learn moves like the shuffle, grapevine, coaster step and mambo step through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Line Dance
programs.description: Are you ready to step up your dance skills with line dancing? Using basic steps to create repetitive combinations, you'll learn moves like the shuffle, grapevine, coaster step and mambo step through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 2
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Develop front and back floats and staying afloat in the water. Learn front and back glide and going underwater. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 1.

programs.name: Jewellery Making
programs.description: Jewellery enthusiasts will love this activity. Here you will use beads, jewellery findings and wire to make a variety of projects. You will also learn how to use the tools of the trade to design your own masterpieces.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Weight Train - Small Group
programs.description: Strengthen your muscles with weight training. Perfect for beginners, you will learn fundamental techniques and a variety of exercises to help enhance your strength, mobility, flexibility and stamina safely. Class size is limited to four participants.

programs.name: Osteo Fit
programs.description: Help build and maintain stronger bones by increasing muscle strength with Osteo Fit. Suitable for individuals with low bone density or osteoporosis, exercises consist of slow-paced, weight-bearing movements with resistance.

programs.name: Painting: Watercolour
programs.description: Create pictures with watercolour paint. Learn basic brush techniques as well as how to choose colours, build layers to show depth and shadow and apply washes for unique effects.

programs.name: Painting: Watercolour
programs.description: Create pictures with watercolour paint. Learn basic brush techniques as well as how to choose colours, build layers to show depth and shadow and apply washes for unique effects.

programs.name: Tiny Tots Swim
programs.description: Would your little one enjoy splashing around and getting comfortable in the water at their own pace? This 15-minute activity provides personal swim instruction with a one-on-one ratio.

programs.name: Adult Swim 1
programs.description: Build confidence in the water one stroke at a time. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres.

programs.name: Computers
programs.description: Designed for first-time users or those with some knowledge, this introduction to computers focuses on how to use a device with confidence. Learn basic computer skills like navigation as well as how to create an email, access the web, create documents and more.Equipment: Use of computers vary by location, please check ahead. You may bring a laptop.

programs.name: Drawing
programs.description: Bring your imagination to life on paper. Learn the basics of how to draw using shapes and colours to create pictures with markers, pencils, and pencil crayons.

programs.name: Functional Fit
programs.description: This conditioning workout is designed to help you build strength to support day-to-day activities. At your own pace, you will practice basic movements like pushing, pulling, bending and squatting to improve your overall functional fitness.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Basketball
programs.description: Focus on developing basketball skills while learning the importance of teamwork and cooperation. Learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play. Develop confidence in both your game and team play as you interact and have fun with peers. Participants must be able to function independently within the program ratio.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Dance: Acro
programs.description: Explore and enjoy this artistic expression that combines dance and gymnastics. In this camp you will learn basic moves from both disciplines and blend them with choreography and your favourite music.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your acro skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp program.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Drama
programs.description: Step into the spotlight and take part in the mysteries of the theatre through drama coaching, set designs and dramatic performances. Learn and practice drama techniques like improv and role-playing, voice projection, expression and script interpretation via skits and exercises.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your drama skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Drama
programs.description: Step into the spotlight and take part in the mysteries of the theatre through drama coaching, set designs and dramatic performances. Learn and practice drama techniques like improv and role-playing, voice projection, expression and script interpretation via skits and exercises. In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your drama skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Recreation Adventures
programs.description: Your recreational adventure begins here. Enjoy activities like sing-alongs, interactive games and arts and crafts while having fun with new friends.
programs.location: The Elms Cmty School and Pool

programs.name: Recreation Discovery
programs.description: Embark on a recreational discovery with a focus on developing skills through creative, active and interactive activities. Programming will include storytelling, theme-based games, crafts, music and physical activity, while having fun with new friends.

programs.name: Science
programs.description: Does science excite you? If so, come explore science through hands-on activities and conduct fun and exciting experiments.

programs.location: Topham Park Cmty Centre and Clubhouse

programs.name: Science

programs.name: Science: Scientific Adventures
programs.description: Jump into the adventures of science. Here, stories, activities and fun and exciting experiments teach basic science knowledge and skills, while hands-on play and the use of your imagination will help you to explore science mysteries.

programs.name: Sculpture
programs.description: Create in 3D! Using a variety of materials like clay, plasticine, paper mache and other recycled materials you will learn to mold, form, carve and caste to create beautiful art.

programs.name: Sewing
programs.description: Come get an introduction to this handy skill. Learn how to read a pattern, cut material, stich by hand or use a sewing machine with the aim of completing a simple project.

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement skills such as agility, balance and coordination. In soccer you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

In soccer you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Soccer: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills though play with recreational soccer. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Adapted Aquatic Games and Skills
programs.description: Playing games and having fun is a wonderful way to practice swimming skills. Designed for swimmers with a disability or special needs, structured aquatic games and activities such as water polo, synchronized swimming and aquatic fitness, provide the perfect oppourtunity to learn and build on basic swimming fundamentals.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Arts
programs.description: Welcome to the realm of abstract art, where linear rules and colour coordination pose no barriers to expression. Using various artistic mediums including paint, clay, food, textiles and water you will create wonderful works of art.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Basketball
programs.description: Focus on developing basketball skills while learning the importance of teamwork and cooperation. Learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play. Develop confidence in both your game and team play as you interact and have fun with peers. Participants must be able to function independently within the program ratio.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Boccia
programs.description: Focus on developing boccia skills while learning the importance of teamwork and cooperation. Learn the basics of this sport, which is similar to lawn bowling, including throwing, aiming, shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play. Develop confidence in both your game and team play as you interact and have fun with peers.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 4: Dolphin
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop front and back swim and sidekick. Learn how to jump into deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Preschool Swim 3.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 4: Dolphin
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop front and back swim and sidekick. Learn how to jump into deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Preschool Swim 3.

programs.name: Recreation Adventures
programs.description: Your recreational adventure begins here. Enjoy activities like sing-alongs, interactive games and arts and crafts while having fun with new friends.
programs.location: The Elms Cmty School and Pool

programs.name: Recreation Discovery
programs.description: Embark on a recreational discovery with a focus on developing skills through creative, active and interactive activities. Programming will include storytelling, theme-based games, crafts, music and physical activity, while having fun with new friends.

programs.name: Science
programs.description: Does science excite you? If so, come explore science through hands-on activities and conduct fun and exciting experiments.

programs.name: Science
programs.description: Does science excite you? If so, come explore science through hands-on activities and conduct fun and exciting experiments.
programs.location: Topham Park Cmty Centre and Clubhouse

programs.name: Science
programs.description: Does science excite you? If so, come explore science through hands-on activities and conduct fun and exciting experiments.

programs.name: Science: Scientific Adventures
programs.description: Jump into the adventures of science. Here, stories, activities and fun and exciting experiments teach basic science knowledge and skills, while hands-on play and the use of your imagination will help you to explore science mysteries.

programs.name: Sculpture
programs.description: Create in 3D! Using a variety of materials like clay, plasticine, paper mache and other recycled materials you will learn to mold, form, carve and caste to create beautiful art.

programs.name: Sewing
programs.description: Come get an introduction to this handy skill. Learn how to read a pattern, cut material, stich by hand or use a sewing machine with the aim of completing a simple project.

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement skills such as agility, balance and coordination. In soccer you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement skills such as agility, balance and coordination. In soccer you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement skills such as agility, balance and coordination. In soccer you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Soccer: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills though play with recreational soccer. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Swim Lessons - Private
programs.description: Do you want to develop specific skills at your own pace? This activity provides personal swim instruction with a one-on-one ratio.

programs.name: Tae Kwon Do - Beginner
programs.description: This activity introduces you to martial arts.  Here you will develop fundamental martial art skills, simple sparring skills, tactical concepts, show willingness to engage in a variety of sport activities and have confidence to try new skills.

programs.name: Tae Kwon Do Learn to Move
programs.description: Learn to move in this martial arts program where you will learn fundamental martial art skills, build confidence, learn etiquette and rules and learn to respect the sport, the instructor and other participants.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Environmental Adventure
programs.description: Get ready to explore the great outdoors. You will learn about and connect with the environment through hiking, crafts and nature games.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on the  environment, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Expressions
programs.description: In this unique visual         arts camp, campers can create, learn and experiment in art studios         housed in a 1912 mansion and carriage house. Campers have an opportunity         to take part in drawing, painting, printmaking, sculpture, arts and         crafts, video and pottery. Also, campers have ample opportunity to enjoy         the beautiful treed grounds and gardens, both making art and         participating in recreation outdoors.Sessions run for two weeks.Please Note: Expressions runs Monday to Friday in two-week sessions, with the exception of statutory holidaysExpressions does not allow registration for children outside of the 8 to 13 age rangeExtended hours are available for an additional charge, from 8 to 9 am and 4 to 5 pmTransportation, lunches and snacks are not included in the registration feeExpressions is a nut-free camp, please do not send any products containing nuts with your child

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Expressions
programs.description: In this unique visual arts camp, campers can create, learn and experiment in art studios housed in a 1912 mansion and carriage house. Also, campers have ample opportunity to enjoy the beautiful treed grounds and gardens, both making art and participating in recreation outdoors.Sessions run for two weeks.Please Note:Expressions runs Monday to Friday in two-week sessions, with the exception of statutory holidaysExpressions does not allow registration for children outside of the 8 to 13 age rangeExtended hours are available for an additional charge, from 8 to 9 am and 4 to 5 pmTransportation, lunches and snacks are not included in the registration feeExpressions is a nut-free camp, please do not send any products containing nuts with your child

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Environmental Adventure
programs.description: Get ready to explore the great outdoors. You will learn about and connect with the environment through hiking, crafts and nature games.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on the  environment, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 4: Dolphin
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop front and back swim and sidekick. Learn how to jump into deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Preschool Swim 3.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 4: Dolphin
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop front and back swim and sidekick. Learn how to jump into deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Preschool Swim 3.

programs.name: Recreation Adventures
programs.description: Your recreational adventure begins here. Enjoy activities like sing-alongs, interactive games and arts and crafts while having fun with new friends.
programs.location: The Elms Cmty School and Pool

programs.name: Recreation Discovery
programs.description: Embark on a recreational discovery with a focus on developing skills through creative, active and interactive activities. Programming will include storytelling, theme-based games, crafts, music and physical activity, while having fun with new friends.

programs.name: Science
programs.description: Does science excite you? If so, come explore science through hands-on activities and conduct fun and exciting experiments.

programs.name: Science
programs.description: Does science excite you? If so, come explore science through hands-on activities and conduct fun and exciting experiments.
programs.location: Topham Park Cmty Centre and Clubhouse

programs.name: Science
programs.description: Does science excite you? If so, come explore science through hands-on activities and conduct fun and exciting experiments.

programs.name: Science: Scientific Adventures
programs.description: Jump into the adventures of science. Here, stories, activities and fun and exciting experiments teach basic science knowledge and skills, while hands-on play and the use of your imagination will help you to explore science mysteries.

programs.name: Sculpture
programs.description: Create in 3D! Using a variety of materials like clay, plasticine, paper mache and other recycled materials you will learn to mold, form, carve and caste to create beautiful art.

programs.name: Sewing
programs.description: Come get an introduction to this handy skill. Learn how to read a pattern, cut material, stich by hand or use a sewing machine with the aim of completing a simple project.

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement skills such as agility, balance and coordination. In soccer you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement skills such as agility, balance and coordination. In soccer you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement skills such as agility, balance and coordination. In soccer you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Soccer: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills though play with recreational soccer. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Swim Lessons - Private
programs.description: Do you want to develop specific skills at your own pace? This activity provides personal swim instruction with a one-on-one ratio.

programs.name: Swim Lessons - Private
programs.description: Do you want to develop specific skills at your own pace? This activity provides personal swim instruction with a one-on-one ratio.

programs.name: Swim Lessons - Private
programs.description: Do you want to develop specific skills at your own pace? This activity provides personal swim instruction with a one-on-one ratio.

programs.name: Swim Lessons - Private

programs.name: Tae Kwon Do - Beginner
programs.description: This activity introduces you to martial arts.  Here you will develop fundamental martial art skills, simple sparring skills, tactical concepts, show willingness to engage in a variety of sport activities and have confidence to try new skills.

programs.name: Tae Kwon Do Learn to Move
programs.description: Learn to move in this martial arts program where you will learn fundamental martial art skills, build confidence, learn etiquette and rules and learn to respect the sport, the instructor and other participants.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Expressions
programs.description: In this unique visual         arts camp, campers can create, learn and experiment in art studios         housed in a 1912 mansion and carriage house. Campers have an opportunity         to take part in drawing, painting, printmaking, sculpture, arts and         crafts, video and pottery. Also, campers have ample opportunity to enjoy         the beautiful treed grounds and gardens, both making art and         participating in recreation outdoors.Sessions run for two weeks.Please Note: Expressions runs Monday to Friday in two-week sessions, with the exception of statutory holidaysExpressions does not allow registration for children outside of the 8 to 13 age rangeExtended hours are available for an additional charge, from 8 to 9 am and 4 to 5 pmTransportation, lunches and snacks are not included in the registration feeExpressions is a nut-free camp, please do not send any products containing nuts with your child
